(born October 31, 1935) is a retired American film and television actor best known as Lt. Matt Reardon in *[Get Christie Love!](/wiki/Get_Christie_Love! "Get Christie Love!")* opposite co-star [Teresa Graves](/wiki/Teresa_Graves "Teresa Graves").

American film and television actor (born 1935)

**Charles M. Cioffi** (born October 31, 1935) is a retired American film and television actor best known as Lt. Matt Reardon in *[Get Christie Love!](/wiki/Get_Christie_Love! "Get Christie Love!")* opposite co-star [Teresa Graves](/wiki/Teresa_Graves "Teresa Graves").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Charles_Cioffi&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Charles Cioffi, who is of [Italian](/wiki/Italian_Americans "Italian Americans") heritage, was born in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"). He attended [Michigan State University](/wiki/Michigan_State_University "Michigan State University"), where he became a member of the [Sigma Chi](/wiki/Sigma_Chi "Sigma Chi") fraternity. His film roles include Lt. Vic Androzzi in *[Shaft](/wiki/Shaft_(1971_film) "Shaft (1971 film)")* (1971), Peter Cable in *[Klute](/wiki/Klute "Klute")* (1971), and Pop in *[All the Right Moves](/wiki/All_the_Right_Moves_(film) "All the Right Moves (film)")* (1983).

Cioffi has appeared on *[Kojak](/wiki/Kojak "Kojak")*, *[Frasier](/wiki/Frasier "Frasier")*, *[Wings](/wiki/Wings_(NBC_TV_series) "Wings (NBC TV series)")*, *[The X-Files](/wiki/The_X-Files "The X-Files")*, *[Thirtysomething](/wiki/Thirtysomething "Thirtysomething")*, *[NYPD Blue](/wiki/NYPD_Blue "NYPD Blue")*, *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*, *[Cannon](/wiki/Cannon_(TV_series) "Cannon (TV series)")*,[[2]](#cite_note-2) *[Bonanza](/wiki/Bonanza "Bonanza")*, and various other series, including *[The A-Team](/wiki/The_A-Team "The A-Team")* (1983) as Gianni Christian, and *[Days of Our Lives](/wiki/Days_of_Our_Lives "Days of Our Lives")*, in which he played Ernesto Toscano. He appeared in several productions both on and off Broadway. He made an appearance on *[Law & Order](/wiki/Law_%26_Order "Law & Order")* as mob boss Frank Masucci. He voiced Chairman Prescott and Adam Fenix in the [Xbox 360](/wiki/Xbox_360 "Xbox 360") videogame *[Gears of War 2](/wiki/Gears_of_War_2 "Gears of War 2")*.
